summary: Doc-only triage. While Lane 2 Batches 1-3 were shipped (Today Continue, Write capture inbox, Train active/history), a parallel agent (Agent B) triaged and shipped Batch 4 as HomeProtocolRegression covering Home protocol template archive/restore. This triage independently selected Home protocols as the next surface but narrowed to active-run step progression — a different sub-behavior. Non-destructive reconciliation: Batch 4 archive/restore (Agent B) stands; this triage's step-progression selection is preserved as Batch 5 with a distinct slice_id. Decision memo added to docs/workflows/ui-regression-plan.md under '### Batch 5 decision'. Queued implementation slice owlory-ui-regression-batch-5-home-protocol-run-step-progression. Patterns and localization layout remain deferred.

contract_status_changes.before: After Lane 2 Batches 1-3 (Today/Write/Train) shipped and Agent B picked Home protocol template archive/restore as Batch 4, the next sub-behavior within Home protocols was unscoped. Active-run step interactions inside home.protocolRun.sheet had no Lane 2 UI proof.
contract_status_changes.after: Batch 5 sub-behavior explicitly selected: Home protocols active-run step progression. Decision memo recorded in docs/workflows/ui-regression-plan.md under '### Batch 5 decision' with a sub-behavior comparison table within Home protocols (step progression, step skip, step revert, schedule windows, template editing). Implementation slice owlory-ui-regression-batch-5-home-protocol-run-step-progression queued with explicit scope (open active run sheet via Today Continue route, tap Complete on first pending step, assert step transitions out of pending), required infrastructure (home.protocolRun.step.<uuid> and home.protocolRun.step.action.complete.<uuid> identifiers, new HomeProtocolRunStepRegression class separate from HomeProtocolRegression), and proof_level target running-app-smoke.

residual_risks: Slice numbering matters because the queue is multi-agent. Reviewers should note that Batch 4 = archive/restore (HomeProtocolRegression, Agent B) and Batch 5 = step progression (queued, this triage).
Active-run step progression is one of several Home-protocols sub-behaviors. Skip, revert, schedule, and template editing remain deferred; each needs its own future triage if prioritized.

The implementation slice will add new accessibility identifiers on the same view that already carries Group-A labels and Agent B's home.protocol.row.* / home.protocol.archived.* identifiers. All namespaces coexist; reviewers should not confuse labels with identifiers.
This slice does not prove anything about running-app behavior, screenshot integrity, device behavior, or TestFlight identity. proof_level is doc-only.
recommended_next_reason: Queued behind this triage with explicit scope and required validations. The supervisor dry-run already selects it as the next eligible slice.
